appflow.flow.CfnAbout:
The `AWS::AppFlow::Flow` resource is an Amazon AppFlow resource type that specifies a new flow. 

**Note**  
If you want to use AWS CloudFormation to create a connector profile for connectors that implement OAuth (such as Salesforce, Slack, Zendesk, and Google Analytics), you must fetch the access and refresh tokens. You can do this by implementing your own UI for OAuth, or by retrieving the tokens from elsewhere. Alternatively, you can use the Amazon AppFlow console to create the connector profile, and then use that connector profile in the flow creation CloudFormation template.

appflow.flow.Description.Description: A user-entered description of the flow.

appflow.flow.DestinationFlowConfigList.Description: The configuration that controls how Amazon AppFlow places data in the destination connector.

appflow.flow.FlowName.Description: The specified name of the flow. Spaces are not allowed. Use underscores (_) or hyphens (-) only.

appflow.flow.KMSArn.Description: The ARN (Amazon Resource Name) of the Key Management Service (KMS) key you provide for encryption. This is required if you do not want to use the Amazon AppFlow-managed KMS key. If you don't provide anything here, Amazon AppFlow uses the Amazon AppFlow-managed KMS key.

appflow.flow.SourceFlowConfig.Description: Contains information about the configuration of the source connector used in the flow.

appflow.flow.Tags.Description: The tags used to organize, track, or control access for your flow.

appflow.flow.Tasks.Description: A list of tasks that Amazon AppFlow performs while transferring the data in the flow run.

appflow.flow.TriggerConfig.Description: The trigger settings that determine how and when Amazon AppFlow runs the specified flow.
